title: 10 best herbal capsules for asthma

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease bronchial constriction, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and asthma symptoms, while also protecting the lungs from damage caused by environmental pollutants.
Bronchodilatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's constituents have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle, allowing for improved airflow and bronchial dilation, making it easier to breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with asthma because they contain a unique blend of herbs that have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The active compounds in these capsules, such as echinacein and cichoric acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and ease breathing congestion. Additionally, the natural antispasmodic effects of the herb help relax bronchial muscles, allowing for easier airflow and reduced wheezing. Regular consumption of Kansas coneflower capsules may provide relief from asthma symptoms and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to the development and severity of asthma.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as echinacoside and cichoric acid, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Polyphenolic Compounds: The polyphenolic compounds in Echinacea angustifolia, particularly echinacoside and cichoric acid, may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to asthma pathogenesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make capsules for asthma because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are also used to make capsules for asthma because they have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to reduce inflammation in the airways.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make capsules for asthma because they have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of echinacea angustifolia roots in autumn when the plant is at optimal dryness.
Clean the roots thoroughly by washing them in cold running water for 30 minutes to remove dirt.
Dry the cleaned roots in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours or use a food dryer.
Grind 500 grams of dried echinacea root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 10,000 rpm.
Fill 500 empty gelatin capsules with 1 gram of the powdered echinacea root each using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with asthma because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to help relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce inflammation. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and coughing, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree capsules can help protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals, leading to improved lung function and overall health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and airway constriction, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.

Inflammation reduction: Maidenhair Tree extract has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the inflammation in the lungs and airways that occurs during an asthma attack, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Vasodilation: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve blood flow to the lungs and airways, making it easier for oxygen to reach the body's cells and reducing bronchospasm.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help relax airway smooth muscles and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called bilobalide, which is thought to help reduce inflammation and improve lung function in people with asthma.

Barks: Some studies suggest that the bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree may also be effective in treating asthma due to its flavonoid content, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve airway function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in autumn when leaves are at peak yellow color.
Dry the harvested leaves in a temperature-controlled environment at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1:1 ratio of the ginkgo biloba powder with gelatin to create a mixture for capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the ginkgo biloba-gelatin mixture and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak capsules helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways. The capsules may also help to relax the muscles surrounding the bronchial tubes, allowing for easier breathing and reducing symptoms of wheezing and shortness of breath associated with asthma. Additionally, English oak has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in English oak, has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and constrict airway spasms associated with asthma.
Vasodilation: The capsules' vasodilation properties help to relax and widen airways, improving airflow and reducing symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Antioxidant activity: English oak's antioxidant compounds help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to asthma development and exacerbation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Quercus robur, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, making it beneficial for asthma management.
Saponins: Saponins in Quercus robur have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway muscles, improve lung function, and reduce symptoms of asthma.

Phenolic acids (Ferulic acid): Ferulic acid, a phenolic acid found in Quercus robur, has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: Used to make capsules due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Collect and clean 30g of dried quercus robur bark from reputable sources ensuring quality and purity standards.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered bark with 30g of cellulose or other natural fillers in a ratio of 1:1 to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the bark-filler mixture using a capsule filler machine or spoon for 10 minutes.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place protected from moisture and light for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with asthma because they contain mucilages, which are natural thickening agents that soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the respiratory tract. When inhaled, these mucilages form a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce airway constriction and excess mucus production, thereby alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with asthma symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbs in marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and bronchial tubes, a common issue in people with asthma.

Expectorant properties: Marshmallow root has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with asthma by forming a protective barrier in the airways, reducing inflammation, and making it easier to breathe.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with asthma by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce the severity of asthma symptoms and prevent airway constriction.
Saponins: Saponins help with asthma by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators, which contribute to airway constriction and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe airways in asthma patients.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high mucilage content and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the airways in asthma patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of powder with 2 grams of gelatin and 1 gram of water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them immediately to prevent drying.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden capsules helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the airways, reducing swelling and constriction. The capsules also contain flavonoids, which have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, littleleaf linden has been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues, including bronchitis and allergies, making it a natural ally for individuals with asthma. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal littleleaf linden capsules can help alleviate symptoms and improve lung function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Littleleaf linden capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of asthma.

Antispasmodic properties: The herb has antispasmodic properties that can help relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing constriction and spasms in the airways, which can make breathing more difficult for people with asthma.

Antioxidant activity: Littleleaf linden capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of chronic respiratory diseases like asthma.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help with asthma by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce bronchial inflammation and swelling, thereby easing breathing.
Flavonoids: Assist in reducing asthma symptoms by their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help in relaxing bronchial muscles and improving lung function.
Tannins: Contribute to alleviating asthma symptoms by their bronchodilatory properties, which help in widening bronchial airways and improving oxygen flow to the lungs.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing asthma symptoms.
Buds: The buds are used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm and soothe the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia cordata flowers and leaves in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed and dried for 2-3 weeks.
Dry the harvested tilia cordata flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried tilia cordata flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the ground tilia cordata powder with 500mg of a carrier vegetable cellulose for 2 minutes.
Fill empty vegetable cellulose capsules with the powder mixture using a powder filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with asthma because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. When taken regularly, it helps to thin mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing inflammation caused by bronchial constriction. Additionally, salicin also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and relax the airways, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function. As a natural alternative to pharmaceuticals, white willow capsules offer a gentle yet effective way to manage asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce inflammation and relax airway constriction in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in white willow bark capsules help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development of asthma symptoms such as inflammation and bronchospasms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White willow bark contains a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to manage their symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin is a phenolic compound that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid is a phenolic acid that has bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their expectorant properties, which help in relieving bronchial congestion associated with asthma.
Buds: They are also used due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which help in soothing airways and relieving asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of salix alba bark in late summer or early fall when resin content is highest.
Dry the harvested bark in a cool well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind 100g of dried bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the ground salix alba powder using a small spoon.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with asthma because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway constriction and swelling. By reducing inflammation in the lungs, frankincense capsules can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the capsules' ability to relax bronchial smooth muscle helps to open up airways, making it easier for oxygen to flow and improving overall lung function. This natural approach has been shown to be effective in reducing asthma attacks and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate bronchial constriction and ease symptoms of asthma.

Immune-modulating property: Frankincense has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the overactive inflammatory response that is characteristic of asthma, thereby reducing the severity of attacks.

Bronchodilatory property: The sesquiterpenes present in frankincense have been found to dilate bronchial airways, which can help relieve wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids (specifically, Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid and Acetyl-β-boswellic acid): These terpenoids help with asthma by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to airway inflammation and constriction.
α-Boswellic acid (α-BA): α-BA helps with asthma by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thereby improving lung function and symptoms.

Boswellic acids (specifically, 11-keto-β-boswellic acid and β-boswellic acid): These boswellic acids help with asthma by blocking the 5-lipoxygenase enzyme, which is involved in the production of leukotrienes, pro-inflammatory mediators that contribute to asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are used due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which are anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are used because they contain a higher amount of boswellic acids compared to the roots, making them a more potent ingredient for asthma treatment.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making them a valuable ingredient in asthma capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried boswellia serrata resin from mature trees under the guidance of a qualified herbalist.
Grind the dried boswellia serrata resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 250g of the ground boswellia serrata powder with 150g of gelatin and 50g of water in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the boswellia serrata mixture using a capsule filler for 20 minutes.
Label and store the boswellia serrata capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with asthma because they contain a natural ingredient that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm down the bronchial tubes and improve lung function, allowing individuals to manage their symptoms more effectively. Additionally, ashwagandha capsules have been found to increase oxygen levels in the body, which can help to alleviate respiratory distress and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms, by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, thus reducing asthma attacks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, a common underlying cause of asthma, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and enzymes.

Bronchodilatory properties: Ashwagandha has been found to relax bronchial smooth muscle, improving lung function and increasing airflow, which can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroid-like compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.

Alkaloids (Withanine and Withaferin A): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce airway inflammation associated with asthma.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, leading to improved asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, they are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: Packed with flavonoids and phenolic acids, they help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with asthma.
Seeds: Containing significant amounts of alkaloids, they are used for their bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects to ease asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 1 kilogram of dried withania somnifera root powder using a digital scale.
Mix the withania somnifera powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a large bowl.
Add 250 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and stir the mixture for 10 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the withania somnifera mixture.
Label and date the filled capsules and store them in a cool dry place for 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with asthma because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways. The capsules also contain antioxidants, such as vitamin C and potassium, which can help protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, dandelion has been shown to increase glutathione levels, a powerful antioxidant that helps to detoxify the body, which is particularly beneficial for individuals with asthma. By taking dandelion capsules regularly, individuals with asthma may experience relief from symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce airway inflammation in asthma patients, easing congestion and breathing difficulties.

Bronchodilation: The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in dandelion may help relax bronchial smooth muscle, widening airways and improving lung function, thereby alleviating symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which are common underlying factors in asthma development and progression.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin is a prebiotic that can help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which may contribute to a decrease in asthma symptoms.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin, taraxasterol is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation in the airways.

Luteolin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, luteolin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their high content of inulin, a natural polysaccharide that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate asthma symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used for their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce asthma symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the collected taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1 week.
Grind 500 milligrams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground taraxacum officinale powder with a capsule filler and add a few drops of glycerin to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with asthma because they contain a compound called quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient is able to relax the airways, making it easier for air to pass through and reducing wheezing and coughing. Additionally, onion capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs, a common symptom of asthma. By taking onion capsules regularly, individuals with asthma may experience improved lung function and reduced symptoms overall.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal onion capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in onion capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, can help reduce airway inflammation and constriction, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.

Mucus-thinning properties: Onion capsules contain mucolytic agents that help break down excess mucus in the lungs, which can make breathing easier and reduce wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur-containing compound, is known for its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation in the airways and relaxing bronchial muscles.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, contributing to improved asthma management.

S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide (SACSO): SACSO, a sulfur-containing compound, has been found to possess bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease asthma symptoms by relaxing bronchial muscles and reducing inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.

Buds: The buds are used because they contain compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.

Stems: The stems are used because they contain compounds like alliin and diallyl disulfide, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that can help relax airway muscles and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Dry the sliced allium cepa in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 4-6 hours.
Grind the dried allium cepa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1000 milligrams of the allium cepa powder with 1000 milligrams of carrier agent such as microcrystalline cellulose.
Use a capsule machine to fill 1000 milligram capsules with the powder mixture.
Store the herbal allium cepa capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme capsules helps with asthma because they contain essential oils that have a natural bronchodilatory effect, helping to relax constricted airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs. The capsules also contain compounds that help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. Additionally, creeping thyme has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the airway lining, providing relief from asthma symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce airway inflammation and bronchospasm associated with asthma.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's mucilage content helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits common in asthma sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and open up airways in asthmatic patients.
Buds: Contains essential oils like thymol, which have natural bronchodilatory properties, helping to relax constricted airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus serpyllum leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two days.
Weigh out one part dried thymus serpyllum leaf and mix it with four parts dried mint leaves and store the blend.
Grind the dried herb mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for ten minutes to ensure maximum extraction.
Combine 500 milligrams of the powdered herb mixture with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled herbal infusion and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with asthma because it has a natural bronchodilatory effect, which relaxes the airway muscles and increases airflow to the lungs. The capsules contain citral, a compound that inhibits the release of histamine, a key player in asthma symptoms. Additionally, lemongrass has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Regular consumption of lemongrass capsules may help alleviate wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma attacks, providing relief for those suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemongrass capsules help to reduce swelling and constriction of airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.

Bronchodilatory effects: Lemongrass contains a compound called citral, which has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, allowing airways to dilate and improving lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have a rich concentration of essential oils and antioxidants that help soothe respiratory issues associated with asthma.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of lemongrass is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate asthmatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them using air circulation at room temperature for 2-3 days.
Pulverize dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powdered cymbopogon citratus with 250mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them securely to ensure freshness.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the airways. The herb's flavonoids and terpenoids work to reduce swelling in the lungs, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for asthma attacks. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, herbal chamomile capsules offer a natural solution for managing asthma symptoms and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in chamomile capsules relax the airway muscles, reducing spasms that can trigger asthma attacks and making it easier to breathe.
Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects help calm the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-related asthma symptoms such as rapid breathing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate bronchial spasms.
Seeds: Contain a high amount of alpha-bisabolol, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Dry matricaria chamomilla flowers in a warm oven at 30°c for 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 10g of ground matricaria chamomilla with 90g of gelatin and 20g of water to create filling mixture.
Stuff the filling mixture into empty hard shell capsules and seal tightly with a gelatin adhesive.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with asthma because of their ability to calm and soothe the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. The antispasmodic properties in lavender work to relax airway muscles, allowing for smoother breathing and reduced wheezing. Additionally, the decongestant effects help to alleviate excess mucus production, making it easier to breathe freely. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, English lavender capsules also reduce stress and anxiety, common asthma triggers that can exacerbate symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce airway inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lavender capsules, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage lung tissue and contribute to oxidative stress in people with asthma.

Sedative and relaxing properties: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is often linked to asthmatic symptoms, allowing for better management of breathlessness and bronchospasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and bronchodilatory effects, making them effective for relieving asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them properly using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the lavender powder with 10 grams of capsules filler such as rice flour in a bowl and stir well to combine.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the lavender mixture using a capsule filling machine or manual technique for 30 seconds per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with asthma because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, rosemary's natural expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further reducing congestion and discomfort associated with asthma attacks. By promoting healthy lung function and reducing inflammation, herbal rosemary capsules may provide a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary capsules' antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common exacerbating factor for asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilatory Effects: Rosemary's bronchodilatory effects can help relax and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in rosemary capsules, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is a key symptom of asthma attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making them effective in managing asthma symptoms.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which have been shown to improve lung function and reduce bronchial constriction in asthmatic patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with two parts of vegetable fiber filler.
Fill 500mg empty capsules with the mixture and seal.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with asthma because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the airways. This can make it easier for people with asthma to breathe and may help to relieve symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. Additionally, fennel capsules may also help to relax the airway muscles, allowing for smoother breathing and reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. Overall, herbal fennel capsules offer a natural way to manage asthma symptoms and improve respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fennel capsules help relieve asthma symptoms by relaxing bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction, and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to asthma attacks and make breathing more difficult.
Expectorant property: Fennel capsules contain natural compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it easier to cough up phlegm and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and bronchoconstriction associated with asthma.
Barks: The barks of the fennel plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including asthma, due to their ability to ease congestion and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds from a trusted source and dry them thoroughly for future use.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 500mg of gelatin and 100ml of distilled water to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with approximately 250-300mg of the foeniculum vulgare-gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine or spoon.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months prior to use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with asthma because they have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate respiratory issues. The herb, also known as Bai Shao, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, peony root has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of asthma. By promoting healthy lung function, Chinese peony capsules can provide relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules contain compounds that can reduce inflammation in the airways, which helps to alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Expectorant property: The capsules can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits associated with asthma.

Antispasmodic property: Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules contain compounds that can relax bronchial smooth muscle, helping to reduce spasticity in the airways and alleviate wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and relax airway smooth muscle, making them effective in alleviating asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like polyphenols and alkaloids, rhyzomes have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, making them useful for asthma treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots from a reputable source measuring one pound or more.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly by rinsing them under cold running water and scrubbing off dirt particles.
Dry the cleaned roots in a well-ventilated area using shade or low heat for 1 week to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried paeonia lactiflora roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 24 hours of drying completion.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the powdered root with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl and fill capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with asthma because they contain a powerful antioxidant compound called resveratrol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways. The anti-inflammatory properties of grape capsules help to open up constricted airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, grape capsules contain other beneficial compounds such as polyphenols and flavonoids that have been found to improve lung function and reduce symptoms of asthma, providing natural relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape capsules contain antioxidants such as flavonoids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in herbal grape capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate airway constriction, swelling, and inflammation associated with asthma attacks.

Mucus-thinning properties: Herbal grape capsules contain mucilages that help thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and airways, reducing respiratory congestion and wheezing commonly experienced in asthmatic individuals.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant contain compounds that help relax airway muscles, reducing inflammation and constriction in the lungs.
Bark: The bark of the grape plant contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bronchial swelling and ease breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of vitis vinifera from healthy vines at dawn when the dew is still present on the foliage.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at a low temperature for 6-8 hours or until brittle.
Grind dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of powdered vitis vinifera with 500mg of gelatin in a small bowl, and add 100ml of distilled water to create a uniform paste.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared herbal mixture, making sure each capsule contains an exact 750mg dose, and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with asthma because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Curcumin also relaxes airway muscles, opening up passages and increasing airflow. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and improving lung function, turmeric capsules provide natural relief for asthmatics, allowing them to breathe easier and live a healthier life.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric, which helps reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic lung diseases like asthma and COPD.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric's ability to inhibit COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes reduces the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory mediators that contribute to asthma symptoms such as airway constriction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate bronchial spasms associated with asthma.
Roots: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds, including volatile oils and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes of high quality from organic sources weighing approximately 500 grams.
Clean the harvested curcuma longa rhizomes thoroughly by rinsing them under running water and wiping dry with a clean cloth.
Cut the cleaned curcuma longa rhizomes into small pieces and dry them in a food dehydrator at 40°c for six hours.
Grind the dried curcuma longa pieces into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve to remove lumps and large particles.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with 200mg of the curcuma longa powder and seal immediately to maintain potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. The herb contains saponins, which have been shown to have a bronchodilating effect, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion. Overall, herbal mullein capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Mullein contains saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve respiratory congestion by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in mullein possess antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to asthma symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Mucilages: The mucilage content in mullein capsules forms a protective barrier on the respiratory tract, soothing and calming irritated airways, reducing inflammation and congestion, and making it easier for the body to expel mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a soothing agent that helps relieve respiratory congestion and inflammation associated with asthma.
Seeds: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers, ensuring proper identification and handling for safety and efficacy.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or sun drying method for at least 48 hours.
Grind the dried verbascum thapsus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered herb with 100ml of distilled water to create a decoction, steeping it for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, transferring the liquid to empty capsules and filling each capsule with approximately 250mg of the verbascum thapsus extract.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for asthma are a natural alternative to traditional medication, designed to alleviate symptoms and improve lung function in individuals suffering from asthma. These capsules typically combine herbal extracts, such as butterbur, garlic, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that help relax airway muscles, reducing wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. For example, peppermint oil has been shown to ease congestion and open up airways, while licorice root extract helps reduce inflammation in the lungs. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals with asthma can experience improved breathing, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall quality of life, allowing them to enjoy activities without being restricted by their condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal capsules are chronic bronchitis, COPD, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Herbal supplements like Butterbur (Petasites hybridus) and Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis) help reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving symptoms of coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Other herbal remedies like Ginkgo biloba and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) also show promise in improving lung function and reducing oxidative stress.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with asthma is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and Echinacea. Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces airway inflammation and congestion. Ginger helps to ease respiratory spasms and relax the airway muscles, while Echinacea enhances immune function and reduces the severity of asthma attacks. Additionally, some herbal combinations may include Mullein and Thyme, which help to soothe and calm the airways, promoting easier breathing and reducing symptoms associated with asthma.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal capsules are beneficial for asthma due to their ability to modulate the secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-1β and IL-8, and anti-inflammatory cytokines like IL-10 and IL-23. This study, which reviewed 51 in vivo experimental systems, found that plant extracts administered to asthmatic animals reduced IgE production, eosinophilic recruitment, and mucus hypersecretion, while enhancing the balance of Th1/Th2 cytokine ratio. Furthermore, the study discovered that herbal capsules can suppress matrix metallopeptidase 9 (MMP-9) and reverse structural alterations associated with asthma, thereby offering potential control activities on asthma symptoms.
